title: Complex picture
summary:
Main
By Michalis Voutsinas
A year ago, in May 2024, an International Monetary Fund (IMF) mission concluded a visit to China, expressing cautious optimism about the country's economic trajectory. The IMF projected that China's GDP growth would remain resilient, estimating a 5 percent expansion in 2024 and a moderation to 4.5 percent in 2025. Inflation was expected to edge up but remain subdued.
